item.title: Naupoto says FICAC investigating departure of Chinese national facing $20M tax fraud probe despite Stop Departure Order
item.description: Rashika Kumar , Mikaele Liga · 23/06/2026 FICAC is investigating the incident in which a Chinese national, who is under investigation by the Fiji Revenue and Customs Service for alleged tax fraud of more than $20 million, left the country despite a stop departure order being issued against him.
item.content:encoded:
Rashika Kumar , Mikaele Liga · 23/06/2026

FICAC is investigating the incident in which a Chinese national, who is under investigation by the Fiji Revenue and Customs Service for alleged tax fraud of more than $20 million, left the country despite a stop departure order being issued against him.

Minister for Immigration Viliame Naupoto has confirmed to fijivillage News that the matter is with FICAC.

He says they are working with FICAC and providing the information required for the investigation.

Naupoto has also confirmed that an internal investigation is underway at the Ministry of Immigration as FICAC's investigations continue.

FRCS has also confirmed that its investigation into the alleged tax fraud is continuing.

The Chinese national, who ran an electronics company in Suva, is the former director of Freesoul Real Estate Development (Fiji) PTE Limited, which was fined $1 million by the Suva High Court for environmental degradation on Malolo Island.

Freesoul lawyer Semi Leweniqila of Toganivalu Legal says he cannot disclose the Chinese national's whereabouts.

He also says the company is now under legal receivership.

item.title: fijivillage Straight Talk live at 7pm tomorrow on HIV cases and the work underway
item.description: A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena. The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.
item.content:encoded:
A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena.

The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.

We have a town-hall set up for this live show where you can also be part of the audience to participate on the show, and ask questions that you will have to send in by 8am next Tuesday.Â

You can send your questions focusing on the HIV statistics in Fiji, where we are at in terms of community-led health outreach, and the clinical prevention rollouts underway.

You can either send in your questions to vijaynarayan@fm96.com.fj or refer to our fijivillage social media pages to put in your questions.

If your question is selected to be asked on the show, you have to be a Fiji Showcase 2026 patron on Wednesday evening, to ask the question in person.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage at 7pm Wednesday.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.Â

item.title: People coming in numbers to access electoral services at Fiji Showcase - SOE
item.description: Supervisor of Elections, Ana Mataiciwa highlighted this to fijivillage News saying they have just completed their Your Election, Your Say or YES outreach project, and thought, why not provide the same services again at Fiji Showcase.
item.content:encoded: Supervisor of Elections, Ana Mataiciwa highlighted this to fijivillage News saying they have just completed their Your Election, Your Say or YES outreach project, and thought, why not provide the same services again at Fiji Showcase.

item.title: Nanuku Resort fundraiser launched to support upgrades at Navua Hospital
item.description: Communities in Navua and surrounding areas will soon benefit from improved healthcare support as a new fundraising partnership with Nanuku Resort Fiji has been launched to raise funds for Navua Hospital. This was highlighted during the launch of the Nanuku Resort Fiji Black Tie Fundraising Event, which aims to improve patient care, hospital services and infrastructure at Navua Hospital through com
item.content:encoded:
Communities in Navua and surrounding areas will soon benefit from improved healthcare support as a new fundraising partnership with Nanuku Resort Fiji has been launched to raise funds for Navua Hospital.

This was highlighted during the launch of the Nanuku Resort Fiji Black Tie Fundraising Event, which aims to improve patient care, hospital services and infrastructure at Navua Hospital through community support and fundraising efforts.

Navua Hospital Board of Visitors Committee member Natalie Marletta says the partnership marks an important step for Navua Hospital, adding that the upcoming Black Tie event will feature prizes and auction items to help raise funds , improving hospital services, purchasing medical equipment and enhancing patient care.

Acting Sub-Divisional Medical Officer Dr Varanisese Tagicaki says the partnership will contribute to strengthening patient care through improved equipment, infrastructure support and better services for the community, describing it as a long-term collaboration to enhance healthcare delivery.

Nanuku Resort Fiji Human Resources Director Charlotte Steele says the resort is honoured to be part of the initiative, stating that healthcare and community wellbeing go hand in hand.

She says the resort has previously supported Navua Hospital, including the installation of privacy gates in the emergency department to improve patient dignity and comfort.

Steele says the resort's support extends beyond this contribution and reflects its commitment to creating positive and sustainable impacts in the communities it serves.

She also acknowledges the dedication of the Navua Hospital Board of Visitors in improving healthcare services for people in Navua and surrounding areas.

Steele encourages the public to support the event, saying healthcare is something everyone will need at some point.

The Nanuku Resort Fiji Black Tie Fundraising Event will be held on August 8th at the Pearl Resort and Spa in Pacific Harbour .

item.description: The counsel for Fiji-Canadian national Joshua Aziz Rahman, whose conviction for possessing $31 million worth of cocaine was quashe
